areas.area: Method and code fidelity to the paper

leaves.label: The All-CNN-C model is implemented exactly as in Table 2 (Section 3.1): every max-pooling layer of base model C is replaced by a 3x3 convolu
leaves.why: models.py build_all_cnn (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each max-pool with a 3x3 stride-2 conv whose output channels equal input channels (conv 96->96 and 192->192), uses only conv+ReLU layers, and ends with conv(192,1x1)+conv(num_classes,1x1) followed by global average p

leaves.label: The three base models A, B, and C from Table 1 (Section 3.1) are implemented: model B as the Network-in-Network variant with one 1-by-1 conv
leaves.why: models.py implements letter A (single 5x5 conv per block + maxpool), letter B (5x5 conv followed by a 1x1 NiN conv per block), and letter C (two 3x3 convs per block), all topped by the conv(192,3x3)+conv(192,1x1)+conv(num_classes,1x1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; metrics.json show

leaves.label: The Strided-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by removing max-pooling and increasing the stride of the convolution layer p
leaves.why: models.py letter 'C' variant 'strided' removes the maxpool (no _maxpool() appended for 'strided') and sets stride=2 on the second 3x3 conv of each block (the conv immediately preceding the removed pool), and the a_strided/c_strided cells ran (a_strided metric=0.8051 in metrics.js

leaves.label: The ConvPool-CNN-C variant (Table 2, Section 3.1) is implemented by placing a dense convolution layer, with the same kernel size as the resp
leaves.why: models.py variant 'convpool' inserts an extra dense 3x3 conv (_conv_relu(96,96,3)/(192,192,3)) before each retained MaxPool2d(3,stride=2) — kernel size matching the 3x3 pooling layer — and c_convpool/a_convpool/b_convpool cells exist in the run outputs listing.

leaves.label: Guided backpropagation is implemented as defined in Section 4 / Figure 1: when backpropagating through a ReLU, values are masked to zero whe
leaves.why: guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (forward_input > 0) * (grad > 0), exactly combining the backprop mask and the deconvnet mask, with no use of pooling switches; run_guided_backprop applies it to the all-conv model (AllCNNModel('C','

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 (Krizhevsky & Hinton, 2009) images are whitened and contrast normalized following Goodfellow et al. (2013), as stated
leaves.why: data.py implements per-sample global contrast normalization (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening with epsilon=0.1 fit on the training set (compute_zca_matrix/ZCAPreprocessor), citing Goodfellow et al. 2013, and cached whitening matrices exist for both datasets (data/zca_cac

leaves.label: The data-augmentation runs use exactly the augmentations of Section 3.2: horizontally flipped copies of all images plus random translations
leaves.why: data.py _augment implements only horizontal flips plus random translation with max ±5 pixels (pad=5 then random crop) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 except for the named online-flip substitution.

leaves.label: The CIFAR-100 experiment reuses the identical All-CNN-C model and identical data pipeline and hyperparameters (learning rate and its schedul
leaves.why: The c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists in the run outputs and reuses the same AllCNNModel('C','allcnn') code (models.py, num_classes parameterized) and the same data.py GCN+ZCA pipeline (zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz cache present), but the hyperparameter-identity claim is not verifiabl

leaves.label: Training logs/configs show all CIFAR networks trained with stochastic gradient descent with fixed momentum 0.9 for a total of 350 epochs, wi
leaves.why: metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 for the cells, and the a_base train_loss_history exhibits discrete drops exactly at epochs ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) with a final small plateau shift after ~300, consistent with the S=[200,250,300] ×0.1 schedule,

leaves.label: Run artifacts show the learning rate γ was searched per model over exactly the fixed set {0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.01}, with results reported for
leaves.why: metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value inside the paper's set), indicating a best-LR selection mechanism exists, but the required evidence of a search over exactly the four-value set is absent from the provided artifacts.

leaves.label: Configs/code apply dropout with probability 20% on the input image and 50% after each pooling layer (or after the layer replacing pooling),
leaves.why: models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every block, exactly matching Section 3.2, but the λ=0.001 weight-decay setting cannot be confirmed.

leaves.label: The ImageNet run trains the upscaled All-CNN-B with 12 convolutional layers (conv1–conv12, Table 6 in the Appendix) for 450,000 iterations w
leaves.why: models.py defines a 12-conv _ImageNetAllCNNB class, but the requirement is an executed training run and the evidence affirmatively shows it was never run.

leaves.label: CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 results are reported as test error (%) on the standard held-out test sets, separately for the no-augmentation and aug
leaves.why: metrics.json reports test_error_pct (e.g. a_base 15.61, a_strided 19.49) per cell with an explicit 'augment' flag and separate aug/noaug cell ids across CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, matching the Tables 3/4 test-error-percent reporting format, with the named coverage gap on the augment

leaves.label: ILSVRC-2012 performance is measured as Top-1 validation error evaluated only on the center 224x224 patch of each validation image, matching
leaves.why: There is no measured ImageNet metric in metrics.json and no executed evaluation path; the requirement is entirely unevidenced.

leaves.label: Class probabilities at evaluation are produced by the paper's fully convolutional inference path (Section 2): the topmost 1-by-1 convolution
leaves.why: models.py ends every network with a 1x1 conv producing num_classes outputs per spatial position and AllCNNModel.forward averages over all positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before the (implicit-softmax) classification — no nn.Linear/fully connected head exists anywhere in the model co

areas.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

leaves.label: Reproduced base model A achieves approximately 12.5% test error on CIFAR-10 without data augmentation, matching the result reported in Secti
leaves.why: metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, lr=0.05) — a working but noticeably weaker result than the paper's ~12.5%, without contradicting any directional claim.

leaves.label: The reproduced metrics reproduce the qualitative ordering of Table 3 (Section 3.2): Strided-CNN-C performs worse than base model C, while Al
leaves.why: Cell outputs for c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug and c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_*/metrics.json), and the analog A-letter trend (a_strided 80.51% < a_base 84.39%) is consistent, but the C-variant nu

leaves.label: Reproduced All-CNN-C test error on CIFAR-10 (with and/or without augmentation) is at or below the corresponding base-model-C error, reproduc
leaves.why: The runs exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json and c_base_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json are listed) but no measured C-variant error values are readable in the provided evidence, so the central comparison is unverified rather than confirmed.

leaves.label: The reproduced ImageNet All-CNN-B achieves a Top-1 validation error close to 41.2% on the ILSVRC-2012 center 224x224 evaluation, with a mode
leaves.why: Only the architecture stub _ImageNetAllCNNB exists in code/models.py with an explicit not-run note, and no ImageNet cell, log, or measured Top-1 error appears in any metrics file.

leaves.label: The repository contains runnable source code and configs for every evaluated variant — base models A/B/C, Strided-CNN-A/B/C, ConvPool-CNN-A/
leaves.why: code/models.py implements all 12 variants (build_all_cnn for letters A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn, MODEL_REGISTRY) with dropout 0.2/0.5 explicit, all 12 cells ran (outputs/prj_*-577651d3/{a,b,c}_{base,strided,convpool,allcnn}_cifar10_noaug), epochs_total=350 and the [200,

leaves.label: Per-run training logs and a machine-readable metrics file (e.g. metrics.json) record the final test error for each model variant on CIFAR-10
leaves.why: Each variant has a per-cell log and metrics.json (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-*/[abc]_*_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json + .log, plus c_allcnn_cifar100_aug and c_allcnn_cifar10_aug), and the visible entries record final test_error_pct/test_accuracy with the producing lr and best_lr fi

leaves.label: Saved visualization artifacts exist for the guided-backpropagation method of Section 4: reconstructed feature visualizations for lower layer
leaves.why: code/guided_backprop.py implements the guided-backprop ReLU rule (GuidedBackpropReLU, run_guided_backprop writing fig_relu_masking.png + guided_backprop.json), but the outputs listing shows only fig_training.png per cell with no guided-backprop artifact present, and the method vi

leaf_scores.justification: metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, lr=0.05) — a working but noticeably weaker result than the paper's ~12.5%, without contradicting any directional claim.

Cell outputs for c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug and c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_*/metrics.json), and the analog A-letter trend (a_strided 80.51% < a_base 84.39%) is consistent, but the C-variant numbers needed to confirm the claimed ordering are not readable in the evidence.

The runs exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json and c_base_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json are listed) but no measured C-variant error values are readable in the provided evidence, so the central comparison is unverified rather than confirmed.
Only the architecture stub _ImageNetAllCNNB exists in code/models.py with an explicit not-run note, and no ImageNet cell, log, or measured Top-1 error appears in any metrics file.

code/models.py implements all 12 variants (build_all_cnn for letters A/B/C × base/strided/convpool/allcnn, MODEL_REGISTRY) with dropout 0.2/0.5 explicit, all 12 cells ran (outputs/prj_*-577651d3/{a,b,c}_{base,strided,convpool,allcnn}_cifar10_noaug), epochs_total=350 and the [200,250,300] schedule is evidenced by the train_loss_history drops at epochs 200 and 250 in metrics.json — but the momentum/weight-decay settings cannot be confirmed from the shown files.

Each variant has a per-cell log and metrics.json (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-*/[abc]_*_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json + .log, plus c_allcnn_cifar100_aug and c_allcnn_cifar10_aug), and the visible entries record final test_error_pct/test_accuracy with the producing lr and best_lr fields (e.g. a_base: test_error_pct=15.61, lr=0.05, best_lr=0.05, dataset/augment flags), making each number traceable to its config.

code/guided_backprop.py implements the guided-backprop ReLU rule (GuidedBackpropReLU, run_guided_backprop writing fig_relu_masking.png + guided_backprop.json), but the outputs listing shows only fig_training.png per cell with no guided-backprop artifact present, and the method visualizes class saliency rather than layer-wise (conv1–3/conv6/conv9) feature reconstructions.

models.py build_all_cnn (letter='C', variant='allcnn') replaces each max-pool with a 3x3 stride-2 conv whose output channels equal input channels (conv 96->96 and 192->192), uses only conv+ReLU layers, and ends with conv(192,1x1)+conv(num_classes,1x1) followed by global average pooling in AllCNNModel.forward (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) producing class logits.

models.py implements letter A (single 5x5 conv per block + maxpool), letter B (5x5 conv followed by a 1x1 NiN conv per block), and letter C (two 3x3 convs per block), all topped by the conv(192,3x3)+conv(192,1x1)+conv(num_classes,1x1)+GAP head, matching Table 1; metrics.json shows a_base/b_base/c_base cells actually ran (e.g. a_base test_error_pct=15.61).

models.py letter 'C' variant 'strided' removes the maxpool (no _maxpool() appended for 'strided') and sets stride=2 on the second 3x3 conv of each block (the conv immediately preceding the removed pool), and the a_strided/c_strided cells ran (a_strided metric=0.8051 in metrics.json).

models.py variant 'convpool' inserts an extra dense 3x3 conv (_conv_relu(96,96,3)/(192,192,3)) before each retained MaxPool2d(3,stride=2) — kernel size matching the 3x3 pooling layer — and c_convpool/a_convpool/b_convpool cells exist in the run outputs listing.

guided_backprop.py GuidedBackpropReLU.backward_hook computes guided_grad = grad * (forward_input > 0) * (grad > 0), exactly combining the backprop mask and the deconvnet mask, with no use of pooling switches; run_guided_backprop applies it to the all-conv model (AllCNNModel('C','allcnn')).

data.py implements per-sample global contrast normalization (_gcn, scale=55) followed by ZCA whitening with epsilon=0.1 fit on the training set (compute_zca_matrix/ZCAPreprocessor), citing Goodfellow et al. 2013, and cached whitening matrices exist for both datasets (data/zca_cache/zca_cifar10_eps0.1.npz and zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz), evidencing it was actually applied.

data.py _augment implements only horizontal flips plus random translation with max ±5 pixels (pad=5 then random crop) and nothing else, matching Section 3.2 except for the named online-flip substitution.

The c_allcnn_cifar100_aug cell exists in the run outputs and reuses the same AllCNNModel('C','allcnn') code (models.py, num_classes parameterized) and the same data.py GCN+ZCA pipeline (zca_cifar100_eps0.1.npz cache present), but the hyperparameter-identity claim is not verifiable from the visible metrics.

metrics.json shows epochs_total=350/epochs_run=350 for the cells, and the a_base train_loss_history exhibits discrete drops exactly at epochs ~200 (0.674→0.558) and ~250 (0.520→0.498) with a final small plateau shift after ~300, consistent with the S=[200,250,300] ×0.1 schedule, but the momentum value itself is unverified.

metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value inside the paper's set), indicating a best-LR selection mechanism exists, but the required evidence of a search over exactly the four-value set is absent from the provided artifacts.

models.py build_all_cnn applies nn.Dropout(0.2) on the input and nn.Dropout(0.5) after each pooling layer or its replacement in every block, exactly matching Section 3.2, but the λ=0.001 weight-decay setting cannot be confirmed.
models.py defines a 12-conv _ImageNetAllCNNB class, but the requirement is an executed training run and the evidence affirmatively shows it was never run.

metrics.json reports test_error_pct (e.g. a_base 15.61, a_strided 19.49) per cell with an explicit 'augment' flag and separate aug/noaug cell ids across CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, matching the Tables 3/4 test-error-percent reporting format, with the named coverage gap on the augmented table.
There is no measured ImageNet metric in metrics.json and no executed evaluation path; the requirement is entirely unevidenced.

models.py ends every network with a 1x1 conv producing num_classes outputs per spatial position and AllCNNModel.forward averages over all positions (x.mean(dim=[2,3])) before the (implicit-softmax) classification — no nn.Linear/fully connected head exists anywhere in the model code, and the evaluated accuracies in metrics.json (test_accuracy=0.8439) were produced through this path.

weak_leaves.justification: Only the architecture stub _ImageNetAllCNNB exists in code/models.py with an explicit not-run note, and no ImageNet cell, log, or measured Top-1 error appears in any metrics file.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: models.py defines a 12-conv _ImageNetAllCNNB class, but the requirement is an executed training run and the evidence affirmatively shows it was never run.
There is no measured ImageNet metric in metrics.json and no executed evaluation path; the requirement is entirely unevidenced.

Cell outputs for c_base_cifar10_noaug, c_strided_cifar10_noaug, c_convpool_cifar10_noaug and c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_*/metrics.json), and the analog A-letter trend (a_strided 80.51% < a_base 84.39%) is consistent, but the C-variant numbers needed to confirm the claimed ordering are not readable in the evidence.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: The runs exist (outputs/prj_0a3202fc187bb692-577651d3/c_allcnn_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json and c_base_cifar10_noaug/metrics.json are listed) but no measured C-variant error values are readable in the provided evidence, so the central comparison is unverified rather than confirmed.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

weak_leaves.justification: code/guided_backprop.py implements the guided-backprop ReLU rule (GuidedBackpropReLU, run_guided_backprop writing fig_relu_masking.png + guided_backprop.json), but the outputs listing shows only fig_training.png per cell with no guided-backprop artifact present, and the method visualizes class saliency rather than layer-wise (conv1–3/conv6/conv9) feature reconstructions.

metrics.json records 'lr': 0.05 and 'best_lr': 0.05 (a value inside the paper's set), indicating a best-LR selection mechanism exists, but the required evidence of a search over exactly the four-value set is absent from the provided artifacts.

metrics.json per_model.a_base.cifar10_noaug records test_error_pct=15.61 after a full 350-epoch run (epochs_run=350, lr=0.05) — a working but noticeably weaker result than the paper's ~12.5%, without contradicting any directional claim.
weak_leaves.area: Result match versus the paper's reported targets
